item.title: 我每天使用的工具 | Tools I Am Using On A Daily Basis
item.description:
我经常被问到我每天使用什么样的工具，以及为什么。 我创建了一个我最喜欢的工具列表，这些工具可以让我保持高效并帮助我实现我想要实现的目标。

保持生产力的一种方法是找到合适的工具，使从事业余项目和创建内容更加方便和容易。我经常使用的工具：🐙 GitHub👩‍💻 VS Code📓 Notion🧑‍🎨 Canva🎶 Spotify（没有音乐的编码?)

我最喜欢的工具

  • 帆布
  • 概念
  • 开发到
  • 无花果
  • 前端大师
  • 推特

帆布

他们最近添加了许多新功能，例如创建演示文稿、录制自己并同时显示自己的视频和演示文稿、在与您的社交媒体帐户（如 LinkedIn、Instagram 等）连接时发布日程安排。

当谈到前端时，VS Code 是我的首选编辑器。 有了许多很棒的扩展，我觉得在我的所有项目中都可以轻松使用它，而不会遇到任何问题。 我喜欢它提供的 DX（开发者体验）。

我不仅使用 GitHub 来存储我的代码，我什至将它用作我在申请工作时分享的作品集🙃

概念

我真的把所有东西都存储在概念上：博客创意、商业创意、年度目标、每月目标，甚至更小的相互连接的待办事项列表、大学课程、工作申请、代码片段等等。

开发到

我每天早上都会浏览 dev.to，阅读带有我感兴趣的标签的最新博客文章。当我开始学习如何编码并成为一名内容作家时，Dev.to 帮助我成长。 我写过的所有文章都在 dev.to 上。 这有助于我更好地理解常见问题及其解决方案。 因为只通过 w3c 文档是🤯。

无花果

一开始我用它来创建缩略图，现在我必须每天在工作中使用它，创建设计系统和原型。 伟大的 DX，易于理解，使用起来很有趣。

前端大师

我经常在 Frontend Masters 观看视频，我喜欢他们的教学方式。 即使他们每个人都有自己的风格，他们总是让我理解。 我对此非常感激！

尽管我仍然有学生订阅，但 FEM 将是我要支付的第二个应用程序，因为它提供了很棒的内容。

由于我有一台 MacBook Air 13”，我的工作效率提高了很多。 我可以通过简单的copy + paste内容从我的 iPhone 共享到 MacBook。

我喜欢 MacOS，由于工作时必须在 Windows 上使用 Lenovo Thinkpad，我能够直接比较它们，并知道最终切换到 Apple 是正确的选择。

还有谁在编码时听音乐？ 我喜欢 Spotify 提供了一种简单的方法来保存您喜爱的音乐、创建播放列表等等。

但我仍然必须切换到 Youtube，有些地方有点烦人。 当涉及到某些电影音乐（电影、动画、动漫）时，内容并不是那么好。 希望会越来越好🙏。

推特

自从我开始编码之旅以来，Twitter 上伟大的技术社区帮助我提高了自己。

我感谢每一个人在我需要帮助时帮助我，并支持我，这是超级激励并让我继续前进的人。 谢谢😇



原文:

I get ask a lot on what kind of tools I am using on a daily basis, and why. I created a list of all my favorite tools which keep me productive and help me reach the goals I want to achieve.

One way to stay productive is to find the right tools that make working on side projects and creating content more convenient and easier.Tools I use regularly:🐙 GitHub👩‍💻 VS Code📓 Notion🧑‍🎨 Canva🎶 Spotify (who codes without music?)

🎨 Figma

My favorite tools

  • Canva
  • Visual Studio Code (VS Code)
  • GitHub
  • Notion
  • Dev To
  • Axe Dev Tools
  • Figma
  • Frontend Masters
  • MacBook Air
  • Spotify
  • Twitter

Canva

Canva has a huge amount of great images, graphics, templates, and so on to find ideas and inspiration to create your own styles.

They recently added a punch of new features like creating presentations, recording yourself and show the video of yourself and the presentation at the same time, post scheduling when connected with your Social Media accounts like LinkedIn, Instagram, and much more.

Canva is actually the first (and until now only) tool I am paying for since I cannot live without any more after 2 years of usage.

VS Code

VS Code is my go to editor when it comes to frontend. With many great extensions I feel comfortable using it for all my projects without facing any problems. I like the DX (developer experience) it is providing.

GitHub

Not only do I use GitHub to store my code, I even use it as my portfolio which I share when applying for jobs 🙃

Notion

I literally have everything stored on notion: blog ideas, business ideas, yearly goals, monthly goals, and even smaller to do lists connected to each other, University classes, job applications, code snippets, and much more.

Notion is providing different kinds of templates, which makes it a lot easier to store and sort everything.

Dev To

I am going through dev.to every morning, reading the latest blog posts with the tags I am interested in. Dev.to helped me grow when I started to learn how to code and become a content writer myself. All articles I have ever written are on dev.to. 💟

Axe Dev Tools

Axe dev tools makes it easy for me to check websites on obvious accessibility issues fast and provides good information on how to solve these issues.

This helps me understand common problems and their solutions better. Because going solely through w3c documentation is 🤯.

Figma

Figma was the first design tool which made it possible that even I can create some sort of UI design which I am proud of. I used it to create thumbnails in the beginning and now I have to work with it every day at work, creating design systems and prototypes. Great DX, easy to understand, fun to work with.

Frontend Masters

I often watch videos at Frontend Masters, and I love the way they teach. Even every one of them has their own style they are always making it possible for me to understand. I am very greatful for that!

Even though I still have a student subscription, FEM will be the second app which I am going to pay for because of its great content provided.

MacBook Air

Since I have a MacBook Air 13“, my productivity increased a lot. I can share content from my iPhone to MacBook by simply copy + paste.

I like MacOS, and since a have to work with a Lenovo Thinkpad on Windows at work, I be able to compare them first hand and know that finally switching to Apple was the right choice.

Spotify

Who else is listening to music while coding? 🙋🏻‍♀️

I like that Spotify provides an easy way to save your favorite music, create playlists and much more.

But I still have to switch to Youtube and some point which is kind of annoying. When it comes to certain film music (film, animation, anime), the content is not that good. Hopefully, it will get better 🙏.

Twitter

The great tech community on Twitter is helping me a lot to improve myself since I started my coding journey.

I am thankful for every single person who is helping me when I need help, and supporting me which is super motivating and keeps me going. Thank you 😇
item.author: yuridevat

item.title: 协作编码实验：最喜欢的评论选择下一行代码，第 5 天 | dev.to codes! Collaborative coding experiment: The most liked comment picks the next line of code, day 5
item.description:
帕斯卡尔·托梅尔

发表于欢迎来到第五天！ 我认为我们已经通过了下一个“行”代码，因此我们将从这里开始使用最喜欢的“一段”代码。 不过，这不是问题。 恭喜@rafaelhashimoto选择了这个大片段。 我不得不稍微调整一下以适应前面的代码。

他的实验受到ProgrammerHumor subreddit 上的一篇帖子的启发，原作者在其中做了这件事：24 小时后最喜欢的评论选择下一行代码。

规则：

  • 没有违反任何适用法律的内容
  • 没有违反社区行为准则、使用条款或隐私政策的内容
  • 不泄露任何人的个人信息
  • 没有恶意软件/勒索软件/病毒/等。
  • 保持文明
  • 到目前为止我们的代码：

     emotions = [ "🥲" , "🥰" , "🥺" , "😫" , "🤬" , "😞" , "😅" , "😊" , "😰" ] how_i_feel_right_now = emotions . sample if how_i_feel_right_now === "😊" letsDoThis () end def letsDoThis # things a person can do that will change his humor what_have_i_done = "🥲" if big_mistakes how_i_feel_right_now = what_have_i_done end def big_mistakes deploy_on_friday () || buy_twitter () end def buy_twitter raise AquisitionError , "I won't buy it" if user_base_has_bots? rescue AquisitionError => e puts e . message true # because you can't step back end def user_base_has_bots? true # well... yes end

    米歇尔·曼纳林—— 一旦被暂停，thormeier 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，thormeier 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发表，thormeier 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 thormeier 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 thormeier 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 thormeier 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Pascal Thormeier

Posted on Nov 12

Welcome to day 5! I think we're passt the next "line" of code, so we'll use the most liked "piece" of code from here on. Not an issue, though. Congrats to @rafaelhashimoto for picking this large snippet. I had to adjust it a bit to fit the previous code.

his experiment is inspired by a post on the ProgrammerHumor subreddit, where the original author did this exact thing: The most liked comment after 24 hours picks the next line of code.

The rules:

  • Nothing that's against any applicable law
  • Nothing that's against the community code of conduct, terms of use, or privacy policy
  • No leaking of personal information of anyone
  • No malware/ransomware/viruses/etc.
  • Keep it civil
  • (To be expanded, depending on the case)

Our code so far:

emotions = ["🥲", "🥰", "🥺", "😫", "🤬", "😞", "😅", "😊", "😰"]
how_i_feel_right_now = emotions.sample
if how_i_feel_right_now === "😊" letsDoThis()
end
def letsDoThis # things a person can do that will change his humor what_have_i_done = "🥲" if big_mistakes how_i_feel_right_now = what_have_i_done
end def big_mistakes deploy_on_friday() || buy_twitter()
end def buy_twitter raise AquisitionError, "I won't buy it" if user_base_has_bots?
rescue AquisitionError => e puts e.message true # because you can't step back
end def user_base_has_bots? true # well... yes
end

(I had to adjust the code a bit for it to be valid Ruby.)

See you in 24 hours!

Michelle Mannering - Nov 2

Once suspended, thormeier will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, thormeier will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by thormeier will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If thormeier is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag thormeier:

thormeier consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging thormeier will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: thormeier

item.title: 数据革命 | The Data Revolution
item.description:
技术革命与常规革命非常相似。 他们突如其来，冷酷无情。 他们把一切都颠倒过来，使以前的惯例变得多余，并开辟了许多新的机会。 众所周知，错过这些创新可能会对业务造成不利影响。 例如，每个人都记得柯达或诺基亚。 然而，只有少数人意识到他们不再存在的原因是他们忽视了智能手机。 然而，您可以通过多种方式预测这些巨大的技术转变。 总有可以注意到的迹象，而这正是我们今天要做的。

失去了巨大的潜力
首先，我们需要了解世界目前的情况。 我们生活在大数据和无处不在的计算时代。 您的手机、汽车、冰箱甚至是您最近购买的智能插座——所有这些小工具一直在收集有关我们的信息，记录我们所做的事情或我们去了哪里。 同时，我们开发了基于复杂神经网络的高级分析算法，可以利用从您那里收集的数据发挥作用。 他们可以预测您的偏好、您的抱负，甚至您的怀孕。 如果仅将您的数据用于良好的事业，那将是完美的。 然而，坏人也从您的数据中受益，使他们追捕它。

仅在 2021 年，就​​有超过1,864 起数据泄露事件。 这使得政府对敏感的用户数据制定严格的规定，试图保护它免受恶意行为者的侵害。 例如，您可能熟悉欧洲的 GDPR 法律。 这些法规控制数据的存储、传输和处理方式，确保不会发生泄漏。 作为一个良好的安全机制，这完全消除了好人使用高级模型改善我们的生活并为每个人提供人工智能助手的机会。

地平线上的光
然而，今天这种情况正在改变。 一些新技术允许以前被认为是疯狂的事情。 您可以将您的敏感数据发送给某人，让他们对其进行处理，这将是非常安全的。 除了您允许他们获得的之外，他们将无法从中获得任何东西。 因此，例如，对于您的健康记录，您可以确定它们仅用于训练新的神经网络以在早期检测癌症，而不是在黑市上出售给一些骗子来勒索您。 您生成的所有其他数据也可以这样做，使它们为一个好的事业工作，而不会给您带来任何风险。

受保护的数据使用所带来的好处是惊人的。 公司可以与其行业合作伙伴安全地结合数据以打击欺诈行为，获得对私人客户数据的安全访问以提供更好的服务，如保险报价或启动真正匿名且安全的应用程序，如没有后门的 VPN 和 DApps。 甚至很难估计它将如何改变我们的生活，但很明显，下一次数据革命正在发生，而且将会是巨大的。

隐私增强技术
正如所承诺的，这里有几句话是关于如何驾驭这场新革命的浪潮。 成为早期采用者之一，并开始学习和构建这些新内容。 目前有几种技术正在竞争，可以大致分为硬件和软件类别。 基于硬件的最容易开始使用，并且是当今最成熟的。

机密计算是一个概念，其中特殊的芯片可以保护敏感的用户数据免受所有人的侵害，甚至是计算机所有者。 计算机以加密方式进行所有计算，可以被视为一个真正诚实、不妥协的演员。 使用机密计算，移植应用程序很容易，因为安全芯片与我们习惯使用的其他芯片没有太大区别。 机密计算单元的唯一缺点是您必须信任芯片制造商，这对某些人来说可能是一个艰难的决定。

基于硬件的替代方案是依赖高级数学和密码学的软件。 在那里，你不需要信任任何人，技术是自我维持的。 最发达的技术是零知识证明，它允许人们在不披露任何用于证明它的机密信息的情况下证明一个事实。 因此，正在开发更先进的技术，例如完全同态加密和多方计算。 它们被证明能够与机密计算的功能相匹配，但仍处于早期阶段，可能还需要 5 年左右的时间才能成熟。

概括
总而言之，毫无疑问，数据革命即将到来。 随着数据收集和分析功能已经到位，私有计算是最后一块拼图。 这些技术将共同动摇我们社会的基础，将先进的人工智能和各种自动化带入我们的生活。 掌握它的关键步骤很简单。 学习并专注于三个领域之一。 然后，找到一种方法来组合公司中的树，因为它们是推进它的无与伦比的组合。



原文:

Tech Revolutions are much like regular ones. They come out of the blue and are ruthless. They turn everything upside down, make previous conventions redundant and open numerous new opportunities. It has been well known that missing out on these innovations can be detrimental to business. For example, everyone remembers Kodak or Nokia. Yet, only a few realise that the reason they no longer exist is that they overlooked smartphones. Yet, there are ways you can anticipate these huge technological shifts. There are always signs which can be noticed, and today this is exactly what we are going to do.

Huge potential is lost
First, we need to understand what the current situation in the world is. We live in the age of big data and ubiquitous computing. Your phone, your car, your fridge and even that smart socket you have recently purchased – all these gadgets collect information about us all the time, logging what we do or where we go. At the same time, we have developed advanced analytics algorithms based on complex neural networks that can do magic with the data collected from you. They can predict your preferences, your ambitions and even your pregnancy. If only your data would be used for the good cause, it would have been perfect. Yet, bad guys also benefit from your data, making them hunt it.

In 2021 alone, there were more than 1,864 data breaches. This made governments make strict regulations around sensitive user data, trying to protect it from malicious actors. You might be familiar with GDPR laws in Europe, for example. These regulations control how data is stored, transferred, and processed, ensuring no leaks happen. Being a good security mechanism, this completely removed the opportunity for good guys to use the advanced models to improve our lives and give everyone their artificial intelligence assistant.

The light on the horizon
However, this is changing today. Several new technologies allow something previously considered insane. You can send your sensitive data to someone for them to do work on it, and it would be perfectly secure. They will not be able to gain anything out of it besides what you allow them to. So, for example, for your health records, you can be sure they are only used to train a new Neural Network to detect cancer in the early stages and not sold on the black market to some scammers to blackmail you. The same can be done with every other piece of data you generate, making them work for a good cause without any risk to you.

The amount of benefits the protected usage of data holds is astonishing. Companies can combine data with their industry partners securely to squash fraud, get secure access to private customer data to provide better services, like insurance quotes or launch truly anonymous and secure applications, such as VPNs and DApps, that have no backdoors. It is hard even to estimate how it will change our life, but what is clear is that the next Data Revolution is happening, and it is going to be huge.

Privacy Enhancing Technology
As promised, here are a few words about how to ride the wave of this new revolution. Be one of the early adopters, and start learning and building with these new. Several technologies are competing right now, which can be broadly split into Hardware and Software categories. Hardware-based are the easiest to start working with and are the most mature today.

Confidential Computing is a concept where a special chip protects sensitive user data from everyone, even the computer owner. The computer does all computations in an encrypted way and can be seen as a truly honest uncompromisable actor. With Confidential Computing, it is easy to port applications, as the security chip is not much different from the other chips we are used to working with. The only drawback of the Confidential Computing unit is that you must trust the chip manufacturer, which for some, might be a tough decision.

An alternative to Hardware-based is Software which relies on advanced mathematics and cryptography. There, you do not need to put trust in anyone, the technology is self-sustained. The most developed technology out there are Zero Knowledge Proofs, which allows one to prove a fact without disclosing any confidential information used to prove it. The ZKP lacks some freedom of expression. Thus there are more advanced techniques under development, such as Fully Homomorphic Encryption and Multi-Party Computations. They are proven to be able to match the functionality of Confidential Computing, yet are still in the early days, likely needing another 5 or so years to mature.

Summary
All in all, there is no doubt that the Data Revolution is coming. With the Data Collection and Analytics capabilities already in place, Private Computations is the last piece of the puzzle. Together, these technologies will shake up the foundation of our society, bringing advanced artificial intelligence and all kinds of automation into our lives. The key steps to be on top of it are simple. Learn and specialise in one of the three fields. And then, find a way to combine the tree in your company, as they are an unbeatable combination to advance it.
item.author: artem_g

item.title: （数组方法第 2 部分）什么是访问器方法？ | (Array Methods Part 2) What are Accessor Methods?
item.description:
迈克尔维尼

发表于几周前，我创建了一篇文章来解释数组方法的类型，我开始谈论数组方法的三种类型：Mutator 方法、Accessor 方法和 Iteration 方法。 如果您还没有看到我建议您查看什么是 Mutator 方法？ 今天我将解释第二种数组方法。

访问器方法

访问器方法和修改器方法之间的区别在于访问器方法不会更改原始数组，它们会返回一个带有更改的新数组，例如：

此方法的返回是一个新数组，其中包含已合并的数组的所有值。

 const animals1 = ['cat', 'dog']; const animals2 = ['fish', 'frog']; const animals3 = animals1.concat(animals2); console.log(animals3); // expected output: Array ['cat', 'dog', 'fish', 'frog']

该函数的返回是通过提供的函数实现的测试的元素。

 const animals = ['cat', 'dog', 'turtle', 'fish', 'duck']; const result = animals.filter(animal => animal.length > 3); console.log(result); // expected output: Array ['turtle', 'fish', 'duck']

我认为您应该知道的其他一些重要的访问器方法：

  • 这就是今天的全部内容！！

一旦暂停，michaelvinidev 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，michaelvinidev 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发表，michaelvinidev 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 michaelvinidev 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 michaelvinidev 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 michaelvinidev 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

MichaelVini

Posted on Nov 12

Weeks ago I create a post to explain about types of array methods, I started talking that there are three types of array methods: Mutator Methods, Accessor Methods and Iteration Methods. In case you haven't seen the last post I recommend you see in What are Mutator Methods?.

Today I will explain about second type of array methods.

Accessor Methods

The difference between accessor methods and mutator methods is that accessor methods not change the original array, they return a new array with the changes, for example:

.concat() - This method is used to merge two or more arrays

The return of this method is a new array with all values of array that were merged.

const animals1 = ['cat', 'dog'];
const animals2 = ['fish', 'frog'];
const animals3 = animals1.concat(animals2);

console.log(animals3);
// expected output: Array ['cat', 'dog', 'fish', 'frog']

.filter() - Method is used to filter an array based on the test implemented by the provided function.

The return of this function is the elements that pass in the test implemented by the provided function.

const animals = ['cat', 'dog', 'turtle', 'fish', 'duck'];

const result = animals.filter(animal => animal.length > 3);

console.log(result);
// expected output: Array ['turtle', 'fish', 'duck']

.includes() - Includes method determines whether an array includes a value and returns true or false as appropriate.

const pets = ['cat', 'dog', 'bat'];

console.log(pets.includes('cat'));
// expected output: true

console.log(pets.includes('turtle'));
// expected output: false

Some others important Accessor Methods that I think you should know:

  • .join()
  • .slice()
  • .indexOf()
  • .lastIndexOf()
  • .toString()

That's all for today!!

Jenuel Oras Ganawed - Oct 17

item.title: JS 上的字符串和数字，药丸...... | Strings and Numbers on JS, pills...
item.description:
娜塔莉亚·卡通达

发表于嗨，这些是一些药丸（小概念部分），您可以在程序员的生活中每天使用它们。

因此，我将所有字符串编译为数字和数字编译为字符串函数和属性，我们可以在 javascript 语言中使用它们。

第一个药丸使用String() ：

 let number="55 86 4232 5267"; console.log("My cellphone number is" + String(number));
  • 第二个药丸使用.toString() : :
 let number="55 86 4232 5267"; console.log("my Number is" + number.toString());

数字转字符串

let width = "90"; let height = "25"; console.log(Number(width) * Number(height));
 let width = "9000"; let height = "80"; console.log( + width * + height);

    *Introduction*
    This article will help you through docker configuration, downloading and building a container image.

    *Prerequisites*

    • You must have installed the VM ware Work Station Pro

    • Downloaded and configured your Ubuntu Desktop.

    *STEP ONE: Pasting URL*

    *STEP TWO: Setting up Repository*

    The next step is to set up the docker repository to enable you install and update docker from the repository.

    • Copy and paste the following command on your terminal:
      *sudo su* The above command will require the password used in setting up the Ubuntu desktop. Once inputted, copy and paste the following commands accordingly:

      Your screen should appear like this

  • Next, copy and paste the paste the command to add Docker official GPG Key


    • Then, set up the repository with the code below

    *STEP THREE:Install Docker Engine*

    • First you will have to update the apt package index with the command below


    • After sucessfully updating, we will go with installing the latest version of your docker engine.

    • Next step is to verify the successful installation of your emgine by running the command below:

    *STEP FOUR: Download a Container Image*

    • Go to *https://hub.docker.com/*

    • Click on Explore and search for Nginx

    • On the left hand side of your screen, copy *docker pull nginx*

    • Paste the copied command in your terminal and hit enter to download the container image in your local environment.

    *STEP FIVE: Start a Container Image*

    • On your terminal, run the command *docker run nginx --detach --publish-all nginx:latest*

    • Run *docker ps* to display the port number

    • Run *ip addr show* to get the ip address of your machine

    • Copy your IP address and port number.


    • Go to your browser and type: *ip address:port* You show see something like the picture below

    *STEP SIX: Build a Container Image*

    Follow the following steps to build the specified image.

    • Create a directory and change to the directory created by running the following command. In this tutorial, our directory will be named *cloud*
      *mk dir cloud*
      *cd cloud*

    • Create a linux text document called *Dockerfile* using the command *vim Dockerfile*
      If done correctly, your screen should appear like the one below

    • Paste the following into the text editor. N/B You can only input anything into the text editor by pressing your "I" button
      *FROM* ubuntu****
      *MAINTAINER (your email address)*
      *CMD ['echo', 'hello world']*

    • Press *esc, *shift + :, then type **wq**** to save your text

    • Exit the text editor by pressing enter

    • Build the container image using the following code
      *docker build -t (name of your choice) .*

item.title: 加密数据库备份并保存在谷歌云平台上 | Encrypt Database Backup And Save On Google Cloud Platform
item.description:
为了改进我的备份策略，我使用 CronJob、OpenSSL 和一个简单的 Bash 脚本将加密备份同步到 GCP。 本教程将解释每个人如何实施我的个人策略。

介绍

在我使用cronjob设置我的数据库备份后，我正在考虑一个很好的解决方案，以便在服务器崩溃的情况下也进行备份。

您可以在此处阅读我如何创建自己的简单备份策略： https ://www.paulsblog.dev/everybody-needs-backups-a-lesson-learned-the-hard-way/

第一个想法类似于rsync作业，它在我的笔记本电脑上运行并每天下载数据库。 但这不是一个非常好的解决方案，所以我考虑了一个包括云存储的解决方案。 因为在我选择它而不是 AWS 时，GCP 有 300 美元的免费积分。

我在这里创建了一个免费帐户，然后创建了我的第一个存储桶。

安装谷歌云 SDK

完成云控制台中的初始设置后，我执行以下步骤将我的服务器连接到谷歌存储。 如果您正在使用 Docker Swarm ，则必须在我的 swarm 中的每个工作人员上重复此过程。 如果你想建立一个 Docker Swarm，你可以在我的另一篇文章中了解它。

下载谷歌云 SDK

 curl -O https://dl.google.com/dl/cloudsdk/channels/rapid/downloads/google-cloud-sdk-353.0.0-linux-x86_64.tar.gz

提取它

tar -xf google-cloud-sdk-353.0.0-linux-x86_64.tar.gz

安装 SDK

 ./google-cloud-sdk/install.sh

安装过程完成后，您必须重新连接外壳！

初始化 SDK

 ./google-cloud-skd/bin/gcloud init

在初始化期间，我必须登录我的谷歌帐户

找到桶

现在机器已连接到 GCP，我可以使用gsutil备份我的数据。

加密

现在我在每台服务器上都有一个谷歌连接，我可以开始创建备份脚本。 但我首先收集了有关如何压缩数据库备份并以加密方式存储它们的信息。 谷歌不应该有我的数据库！ 我知道：妄想症

我选择了一个使用系统上的密码文件（我也下载到我的机器上）的加密程序。 所以我必须创建一个安全密码并将其导出到一个变量中，然后该变量可以在脚本中使用。

在文件中生成随机密码：

 head -c 100 /dev/urandom | strings -n1 | tr -d '[:space:]' | head -c 40 >> ~/.pass

当我在 shell 中时，将文件导出到 .bashrc 以使用它：

 echo "export PASS=~/.pass" >> ~/.bashrc && source ~/.bashrc

使文件“安全”：

使用这些.pass文件，我可以使用以下命令加密和压缩文件夹：

 tar czf - . | openssl enc -e -aes-256-cbc -pbkdf2 -iter 100000 -salt -out archive.enc -pass env :PASS

要解密我必须使用：

 openssl enc -d -aes-256-cbc -pbkdf2 -iter 100000 -salt -in archive.enc -pass env :PASS | tar zxf - --directory backup_restore

创建备份 shell 脚本

最后一步是真正创建一个shell-script ，然后在cronjob中执行。 经过研究和测试，创建了这个脚本：

 #!/bin/bash TIME = $( date +%Y-%m-%d ) HOST = $( hostname ) BACKUPSAVEPATH = /YOUR_BACKUP_STORAGE_PATH/backups_encrypted/ BACKEDUPFILE = $BACKUPSAVEPATH /db-backups- $HOST - $TIME .enc TARGET = gs://YOUR_BUCKET_HERE cd /YOUR_BACKUP_STORAGE_PATH/ || exit 1 if [ -z " $( ls -A . ) " ] ; then exit 1 else tar czf - . | openssl enc -e -aes-256-cbc -pbkdf2 -iter 100000 -salt -out $BACKEDUPFILE -pass env :PASS /PATH_TO_GOOGLE_SDK_FOLDER/google-cloud-sdk/bin/gsutil -m rsync -r $BACKUPSAVEPATH $TARGET || exit 1 rm -rf /YOUR_BACKUP_STORAGE_PATH/backups/ * fi

重要的是要知道您是否阅读/使用此文件：

  1. 将YOUR_BACKUP_STORAGE_PATH替换为您存储备份的路径。 此文件夹必须有两个子目录： backups_encrypted和backups
  2. 使用gsutil ls找到它
  3. 现在可以在cronjob中使用此脚本：

     30 1 * * 6 export PASS = /root/.pass ; /bin/sh /PATH_TO_SCRIPT/SCRIPTNAME.sh

    这个cronjob在每个星期六的 1:30 执行。 使用crontab.guru创建crontab

    非常重要：您必须在执行脚本之前导出PASS变量，因为cronjob不会在您作为用户的同一 bash 中执行，然后先前导出的PASS变量不存在

    也非常重要：请记住在 swarm 中的每个工作节点上重新创建cronjob （如果您使用 Docker Swarm）。 否则，数据将丢失。

    结语

    在这个简单的 How-To 中，我解释了如何使用 Google Cloud SDK 在我的 Google Bucket 中保存一个加密的 zip 文件。

    我有另一个教程解释了如何将文件上传到亚马逊。 您可以从本教程中复制加密部分并将其与上传到 S3 的文件结合起来： https ://www.paulsblog.dev/how-to-backup-your-files-to-aws-s3/

    我希望这篇文章对您有所帮助，并且还能够将您宝贵的备份保存在云中。 我很想听听您的想法，如果您有任何问题，请与我联系。

    本文最初发表在我的博客https://www.paulsblog.dev/encrypt-database-backup-and-save-on-google-cloud-platform/

    请随时在我的个人博客、 Medium 、 LinkedIn 、 Twitter和GitHub 上与我联系。

    你觉得这篇文章有价值吗？ 想支持作者？ 你可以在给我买杯咖啡或Ko-Fi上赞助我。 此外，您可以通过注册我的网站成为免费或付费会员。 请参阅贡献页面以了解所有（免费或付费）方式来表示感谢！

    照片由panumas nikhomkhai / Pexels 拍摄



原文:

Paul Knulst  in  Programming • Dec 7, 2021 • 4 min read

To improve my backup strategy I add syncing encrypted backups to GCP with a CronJob, OpenSSL, and a simple Bash script. This Tutorial will explain how everyone can implement my personal strategy.

Introduction

After I set up my database backup using a cronjob I was thinking about a good solution to have also backups in case of a server crash.

You can read how I created my own simple backup strategy here: https://www.paulsblog.dev/everybody-needs-backups-a-lesson-learned-the-hard-way/

The first idea was something like a rsync job which runs on my laptop and downloads the DB every day. But this is not a really good solution so I thought about one including cloud storage. Because GCP has $300 in free credits at the moment I choose it over AWS.

I created a free account here and then I created my first Storage Bucket.

Installing Google Cloud SDK

After the initial setup within the cloud console was done I performed the following steps to connect my server to the google storage. If you are working with a Docker Swarm this procedure has to be repeated on EVERY worker within my swarm. If you want to set up a Docker Swarm, you can read about it in another article from me.

Downloading Google Cloud SDK

curl -O https://dl.google.com/dl/cloudsdk/channels/rapid/downloads/google-cloud-sdk-353.0.0-linux-x86_64.tar.gz

Extract it

tar -xf google-cloud-sdk-353.0.0-linux-x86_64.tar.gz

Install the SDK

./google-cloud-sdk/install.sh

After the installation process is done you have to reconnect the shell!

Initialize the SDK

./google-cloud-skd/bin/gcloud init

During the initialization, I had to login into my google account

Find the bucket

Now the machine is connected to GCP and I can use gsutil to backup my data.

Encryption

Now that I have a google connection on every server I could start to create the backup script. But I first gathered information on how to zip the database backups and store them in an encrypted way. Google should not have my database! I know: paranoia

I choose a procedure for encryption that uses a password file on the system (which I also downloaded to my machine). So I had to create a secure password and export it into a variable which then can be used within a script.

Generate a random password in a file:

head -c 100 /dev/urandom | strings -n1 | tr -d '[:space:]' | head -c 40 >> ~/.pass

Export file to .bashrc to use it while I am within the shell:

echo "export PASS=~/.pass" >> ~/.bashrc && source ~/.bashrc

Make file “safe”:

With these .pass file I can encrypt and zip a folder with:

tar czf - . | openssl enc -e -aes-256-cbc -pbkdf2 -iter 100000 -salt -out archive.enc -pass env:PASS

To decrypt I have to use:

 openssl enc -d -aes-256-cbc -pbkdf2 -iter 100000 -salt -in archive.enc -pass env:PASS | tar zxf - --directory backup_restore

Creating the backup shell script

The last step was to really create a shell-script which will then be executed within a cronjob. After researching and testing this script was created:

#!/bin/bash
TIME=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)
HOST=$(hostname)
BACKUPSAVEPATH=/YOUR_BACKUP_STORAGE_PATH/backups_encrypted/
BACKEDUPFILE=$BACKUPSAVEPATH/db-backups-$HOST-$TIME.enc
TARGET=gs://YOUR_BUCKET_HERE cd /YOUR_BACKUP_STORAGE_PATH/ || exit 1
if [ -z "$(ls -A .)" ]; then exit 1
else tar czf - . | openssl enc -e -aes-256-cbc -pbkdf2 -iter 100000 -salt -out $BACKEDUPFILE -pass env:PASS /PATH_TO_GOOGLE_SDK_FOLDER/google-cloud-sdk/bin/gsutil -m rsync -r $BACKUPSAVEPATH $TARGET || exit 1 rm -rf /YOUR_BACKUP_STORAGE_PATH/backups/*
fi

Important to know if you read/use this file:

  1. Replace YOUR_BACKUP_STORAGE_PATH with your path where you store your backups. This folder has to have two subdirectories: backups_encrypted and backups
  2. YOUR_BUCKET_HERE should be replaced with your bucket which you want to use. Find it with gsutil ls
  3. PATH_TO_GOOGLE_SDK_FOLDER is the folder in which you installed the Google Cloud SDK

This script can now be used within a cronjob:

30 1 * * 6 export PASS=/root/.pass; /bin/sh /PATH_TO_SCRIPT/SCRIPTNAME.sh

This cronjob is executed at 1:30 every Saturday. Use crontab.guru for crontab creation

VERY IMPORTANT: You have to export the PASS variable before executing the script because a cronjob is not executed within the same bash in which you are as a user and then the prior exported PASS variable does not exist

ALSO VERY IMPORTANT: Remember to recreate the cronjob on every worker node within the swarm (if you use a Docker Swarm). Otherwise, data will be lost.

Closing Notes

Within this simple How-To, I explained how I used Google Cloud SDK to save an encrypted zip file in my Google Bucket here.

I have another Tutorial that explains How you can upload files to Amazon. You can copy the encryption part from this tutorial and combine it with the file upload to S3: https://www.paulsblog.dev/how-to-backup-your-files-to-aws-s3/

I hope you find this article helpful and are also able to save your valuable backup within the Cloud. I would love to hear your thoughts and if you have any questions please contact me.

This article was originally published on my blog at https://www.paulsblog.dev/encrypt-database-backup-and-save-on-google-cloud-platform/

Feel free to connect with me on my personal blog, Medium, LinkedIn, Twitter, and GitHub.

Did you find this article valuable? Want to support the author? (... and support development of current and future tutorials!). You can sponsor me on Buy Me a Coffee or Ko-Fi. Furthermore, you can become a free or paid member by signing up to my website. See the contribute page for all (free or paid) ways to say thank you!

item.author: paulknulst

item.title: 掌握 CSS 选择器的简单方法（第 1 部分） | Master Css Selectors The Easy Way (part 1)
item.description:
所以你在这里学习 css 选择器。 所以问题出现了为什么我们需要选择器

在 CSS 中，选择器用于定位我们想要设置样式的网页上的 HTML 元素。

所以让我用代码示例来解释一下。

我们想改变“P”元素的背景颜色最简单的方法是选择一个元素。

结果

我们选择了一个元素并改变了它的背景颜色，但我们可以用这种方式做所有复杂的事情吗？

类选择器

这是一个让我们的生活更轻松的解决方案。

我们的客户想要订购“饺子”，因此我们将在 ul 的第二个孩子中添加类属性并将其背景颜色更改为绿色作为订购的食物。

哦，不，我们的客户改变了主意，他想要更多的食物，他想要“麻婆豆腐”也幸运的是类选择器来拯救我们，我们也必须在该元素中添加“订单”类。

注意类总是以“.”为目标。 在 CSS 中

结果

所以我们了解了类是如何工作的，但是“id”呢？为什么？ 注意id 在 css 中总是以“#”为目标

结果

但是如果我们想将相同的 id 添加到其他元素** 我们不能 **
但我们可以将同一个类添加到多个元素，所以我认为这显然是有意义的。

但

下一个
假设我们需要设置一个按钮的样式，但不是一个按钮 3 个按钮所有按钮都有相似的样式，但第三个按钮有点不同，所以让我们看看如何使用链式选择器来实现这一点


所以在上面的例子中，我们可以清楚地看到链式选择器如何在不同的情况下帮助我们。

让我们看另一个例子来更清楚这个概念

这里我们的目标是定位具有“bg_black 和 text_white”类的 li 元素

在这个片段中，我们在整个页面中告诉任何具有“bg_black 和 text_white”类的 'li' 元素会更改其背景颜色和文本颜色

在同一个片段中，我们在上面使用了一些更改，如果我们想要定位所有“li”元素，那么我们通过直接子选择器完成此操作。


结果

笔记

不选择不是直接孩子的第 3 li。

相邻兄弟选择器

悬停选择器
当我们将鼠标悬停在任何元素上时，悬停是中继简单选择器，它将应用我们想要的 CSS

选择器之前

后选择器与前选择器完全相反，因此其继电器简单

所以我们现在可以清楚地看到结果按钮在蓝色框之前。

所以今天我们学习了一些 CSS 选择器，如果你想以这种初学者友好的方式了解更多信息，请保持联系谢谢。



原文:

so you are here to learn about css selectors. So question arise why we need selectors

In CSS, selectors are used to target the HTML elements on our web pages that we want to style.

so let me explain with code examples.

_Scenario _

we wanna change background color of "P" element the easiest way is to select an element.

Result

we selected an element and change its background color but we can do all sophisticated stuff in this way?

_Introducing ID and class selectors _

class selector

here's a solution to make our lives a little bit easier.

our customer wanna order "Dumplings" so we will add class attribute in second child of ul and change its background color green as ordered food.

_result _

oh no our customer changed mind and he want more food he want "Ma Po Tofu" also fortunately class selector come to save us we have to just add "orders" class in that element also.

NOTE classes always targeted with "." in css

result

so we get the idea of how classes work but what about "id's" and why? id's here when classes can do everything let's talk about that.

_Id selector _

id is simple just we can target one element with one id I mean id will be always unique we can't use it with multiple elements
NOTE id's always targeted with "#" in css

result

But if we want add same id to other element** we cant **
but we can add same class to multiple elements so i think it'll make sense obviously .

*So these are most basic selectors *

BUT

_if you wanna become css guru bear me more _

Next one
*_ chained selector_ *
lets assume we have requirement to style a button but not one button 3 buttons all buttons have similar styling but third one has bit different so let see how we can accomplish this with chained selectors


so in above example we can clearly see how chained selectors can help us in different situations.

*result *


let see another example to clear this concept more

here our goal is to target li element which have " bg_black and text_white" classes

_Css will be _

*result *

in this snippet we are telling in whole page any 'li' element which have "bg_black and text_white" classes change its background color and text color

_Direct Child Selector _


in same snippet with some changes we used above what if we want to target all "li" element so we accomplish this via Direct Child Selector.


result

Note

3rd li which is not direct child is not selected.

Adjacent Sibling Selector

The + selector is used to select an element that is directly after another specific element.


*result *

Hover selector
hover is relay simple selector when we mouse over on any element it'll apply CSS which we want

_result _

Before Selector

before selector is pseudo selector which inserts something before the content of each selected element.

_result _

_After selector _
After selector is completely opposite of before selector so its relay simple

_ result _

so we can clear see result now button is before blue box.

So today we learn few css selectors if you want to learn more in this beginner friendly way stay connected Thanks.
item.author: waqasongithub

item.title: 容器化全栈应用 | Containerize a full-stack app
item.description:
在本教程中，您将学习如何使用 Dockerfile 文件将全栈应用程序 (React + Django) 容器化，设置 Docker Compose 以运行这两个应用程序并使用 nginx 代理将前端应用程序 (react) 路由到您的实例公共 IP 地址。

这将会非常好玩

创建 exc2 实例 (vm)

克隆仓库

安装泊坞窗

注销并重新登录以添加用户

添加密码 = sudo passwd

现在将 repo 克隆到实例中

您现在将创建 Dockerfile

须藤纳米 Dockerfile

粘贴下面

# ==== CONFIGURE ===== # Use a Node 16 base image FROM node:16-alpine # Set the working directory to /frontend inside the container WORKDIR /frontend # Copy frontend files COPY . . # ==== BUILD ===== # Install dependencies (npm ci makes sure the exact versions in the lockfile gets installed) RUN npm i # Build the app RUN npm run build # ==== RUN ======= # Set the env to "production" ENV NODE_ENV production # Expose the port on which the app will be running (3000 is the default that `serve` uses) EXPOSE 3000 # Start the app CMD [ "npx", "serve", "build" ]
  • 谈谈如何保存和退出nano

现在您将从 docker 文件构建 docker 映像

##docker build -t (give the image a name) . docker build -t frontend .

要查看构建的图像，请运行

或 docker ps 确认容器正在运行

现在您需要将图像推送到 docker hub

 docker login ## tag the image ##docker tag imagename username/tag docker tag frontend favourd205/frontend docker push favourd205/frontend

转到您的 Docker Hub 存储库，您应该在那里看到您的图像。

现在 cd 回到 api 文件夹

这是保存 Django 应用程序的文件夹。 您还需要在此处创建一个 Dockerfile 来为后端构建映像。

须藤纳米 Dockerfile

将以下内容复制并粘贴到其中

FROM python:3.8-alpine ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE 1 ENV PYTHONBUFFERED=1 RUN mkdir /backend WORKDIR /backend COPY . /backend/ RUN pip install -r requirements.txt EXPOSE 8000 CMD ["python", "manage.py", "runserver", "0.0.0.0:8000"]

现在构建图像

docker build -t backend .

然后推送图片

现在要运行你需要 docker compose 的容器，在 vm 上安装 docker compose

安装后，创建一个 docker-compose.yaml 文件并将以下内容粘贴到其中

sudo nano docker-compose.yaml

在文件里面

version: "3" services: frontend: image: favourd205/frontend:latest container_name: frontend ports: - 3000:3000 depends_on: - backend backend: image: favourd205/backend:latest container_name: backend command: python manage.py runserver 0.0.0.0:8000 ports: - "8000:8000"

保存后运行

docker-compose up -d or docker-compose up --detach

这应该运行并调出两个容器图像

在分配的端口上导航到您的实例 IP 地址，您的应用程序应该正在运行

实例IP：3000

和实例：8000

现在你应该为反向代理安装 nginx

 sudo apt update sudo apt install nginx
 Confirm that nginx is running sudo service nginx status or sudo systemctl status nginx

对于反向代理，进入 nginx 目录

现在可以看到所有的 nginx 文件了，我们需要编辑 nginx.conf 文件

删除文件中的所有配置并使用以下内容

events{ worker_connections 1024; } http { keepalive_timeout 5; upstream frontend { keepalive 50; server 34.207.251.1; } server { listen 80; location / { proxy_set_header X-Real-lP $remote_addr; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme; proxy_set_header Host $host; proxy_pass http://34.207.251.1:3000; } } }

确认以上配置语法正确

它应该返回成功

现在重新加载nginx

 sudo service nginx reload


原文:

In this tutorial you would learn how to containerize a full stack application (React + Django) using Dockerfile files, setup Docker Compose to run both applications and use nginx proxy to route the frontend application (react) to your instance public IP address.

This is going to be fun

GitHub Repo

Create an exc2 instance (vm)

sudo apt update
sudo apt upgrade

Clone the repo

Install docker

https://www.digitalocean.com/community/tutorials/how-to-install-and-use-docker-on-ubuntu-20-04

Log out and log back in to add the user

To add a password = sudo passwd

Now clone the repo into the instance

cd into it , then cd into frontend

You will create your Dockerfile now

sudo nano Dockerfile

paste the below

# ==== CONFIGURE =====
# Use a Node 16 base image
FROM node:16-alpine
# Set the working directory to /frontend inside the container
WORKDIR /frontend
# Copy frontend files
COPY . .
# ==== BUILD =====
# Install dependencies (npm ci makes sure the exact versions in the lockfile gets installed)
RUN npm i
# Build the app                                                                                         
RUN npm run build
# ==== RUN =======
# Set the env to "production"                                                                           
ENV NODE_ENV production
# Expose the port on which the app will be running (3000 is the default that `serve` uses)
EXPOSE 3000
# Start the app                                                                                        
CMD [ "npx", "serve", "build" ]
  • talk about how to save and exit from nano

Now you would build the docker image from the docker file

##docker build -t (give the image a name) .

docker build -t frontend .

To see the built image, run

or docker ps to confirm container is running

Now you need to push the image to docker hub

docker login

## tag the image
##docker tag imagename username/tag

docker tag frontend favourd205/frontend

docker push favourd205/frontend

Go to your Docker Hub repository and you should see your image there.

Now cd back to the api folder

This is the folder which hold the Django application. You also need to create a Dockerfile here to build an image for the backend.

sudo nano Dockerfile

copy and paste the below into it

FROM python:3.8-alpine

ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE 1

ENV PYTHONBUFFERED=1

RUN mkdir /backend

WORKDIR /backend

COPY . /backend/

RUN pip install -r requirements.txt

EXPOSE 8000

CMD ["python", "manage.py", "runserver", "0.0.0.0:8000"]

Now build the image

docker build -t backend .

=====remember the virtual environment thing

then push the image

Now to run the comtainers you would need docker compose, install docker compose on the vm

After installation, create a docker-compose.yaml file and paste the below into it

sudo nano docker-compose.yaml

inside the file


version: "3"

services:
    frontend:
        image: favourd205/frontend:latest
        container_name: frontend
        ports:
          - 3000:3000
        depends_on: 
          - backend  

    backend:
        image: favourd205/backend:latest
        container_name: backend
        command: python manage.py runserver 0.0.0.0:8000
        ports:
            - "8000:8000"

After saving, run

docker-compose up -d 

or 

docker-compose up --detach

=====remember to allow all traffic

This should run and bring up the two container images

Navigate to your instance ip adress on the assigned ports and your applications should be running

instanceIp:3000

and instance:8000

Now you should install nginx for reverse proxy

sudo apt update
sudo apt install nginx

Confirm that nginx is running

sudo service nginx status

or 

sudo systemctl status nginx

For reverse proxy, enter nginx directory

Now you can see all nginx files, we need to edit the nginx.conf file

delete all the configurations in the file and use the below

events{
    worker_connections 1024;
}
http { 
    keepalive_timeout 5;
        upstream frontend {
            keepalive 50;
            server 34.207.251.1;


}
server {
   listen 80;
location / {
        proxy_set_header  X-Real-lP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header  X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header  X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
        proxy_set_header        Host $host;
        proxy_pass http://34.207.251.1:3000;
}
}
}

Confirm the above configuration syntax is correct

It should return successful

Now reload nginx

sudo service nginx reload
item.author: danielfavour

item.title: Componente Composto com React | Componente Composto com React
item.description:
简介

组件

第 1 部分 - Organizando a casa

组件排>卡

第 2 部分 - Criando o componente 卡

没有 index.tsx, temos a regra de negócio aplicada, criei um estado para gerenciar se os componentses filhos estão abertos ou fechados e criei uma função que tem o papel de converter o valor do estado。

索引.tsx

 import { createContext, useState } from "react"; import { CardProps, CardPropsContext } from "./props"; import { CardContainer } from "./style"; export const CardContext = createContext({} as CardPropsContext); export const Card = ({ children, ...props }: CardProps) => { const [open, setOpen] = useState(false); function handleClose() { setOpen((e) => !e); } return ( <CardContext.Provider value={{ isOpen: open, handleClose: handleClose }}> <CardContainer {...props}>{children}</CardContainer> </CardContext.Provider> ); };

样式化的.tsx

 import styled from "styled-components"; export const CardContainer = styled.div` display: flex; align-items: center; background-color: #f4f4f4; padding: 8px; justify-content: space-between; border-radius: 4px; flex-direction: column; gap: 16px; `;

道具.ts

 import React from "react"; export interface CardProps { Avatar?: React.ReactNode; children: React.ReactNode; } export interface CardPropsContext { isOpen: boolean; handleClose: () => void; }

第 3 部分 - Criando o componente 按钮

索引.tsx

 import { useContext } from "react"; import { CardContext } from ".."; import { Button } from "./style"; import { BsChevronDown, BsChevronUp } from "react-icons/bs"; export const CardButton = () => { const context = useContext(CardContext); return ( <Button onClick={context.handleClose}> {context.isOpen ? <BsChevronUp /> : <BsChevronDown />} </Button> ); };

样式.tsx

 import styled from "styled-components"; export const Button = styled.button` cursor: pointer; border: 0; width: 30px; height: 30px; border-radius: 100px; &:hover { background-color: #00000005; } `;

第 4 部分 - Finalizando os componentses filhos

索引.tsx

 import { useContext } from "react"; import { CardContext } from ".."; import { CardBodyStyle } from "./style"; export const CardBody: React.FC = ({ children }) => { const context = useContext(CardContext); return context.isOpen ? <CardBodyStyle>{children}</CardBodyStyle> : <></>; };

样式.tsx

 import styled from "styled-components"; export const CardBodyStyle = styled.div` display: flex; flex-direction: column; background: #e1e1e180; width: 100%; min-height: 40px; `;

第 5 部分 - Voltando e finalizando componente pai

索引.tsx

 import { createContext, useState } from "react"; import { CardButton } from "./cardButton"; import { CardBody } from "./cardBody"; import { CardProps, CardPropsContext } from "./props"; import { CardContainer } from "./style"; export const CardContext = createContext({} as CardPropsContext); export const Card = ({ children, ...props }: CardProps) => { const [open, setOpen] = useState(false); function handleClose() { setOpen((e) => !e); } return ( <CardContext.Provider value={{ isOpen: open, handleClose: handleClose }}> <CardContainer {...props}>{children}</CardContainer> </CardContext.Provider> ); }; Card.Button = CardButton; Card.Body = CardBody;

第 6 部分 - Aplicando o componente

没有app.tsx程序。

应用程序.tsx

 import { Card } from "./components/card"; import "./styles.css"; export default function App() { return ( <div className="App"> <Card> <div style={{ display: "flex", flexDirection: "row", alignItems: "center", width: "100%", gap: 16 }} > <div style={{ marginLeft: "auto" }}> <Card.Button /> </div> </div> <Card.Body> <h1>Teste</h1> </Card.Body> </Card> </div> ); }

菲利尼桑多

Galera, agradeço a leitura deste post, espero ter ajudado este é o Codepen prometido , caso tenham duvidas podem me chama no Linkedin que respondo sempre que puder e me sigam no Github !, logo mais terá um projeto open source bacana lá!! Galera, agradeço a leitura deste post, espero ter ajudado este é o Codepen prometido , caso tenham duvidas podem me chama no Linkedin que respondo semper que puder e me sigam no Github !, logo mais terá um projeto open source bacana la!! Até mais e bons códigos pra vcs!!! Até mais e bons códigos pra vcs!!!



原文:

Olá, me chamo Raul, atualmente sou dev front-end sênior e quero compartilhar com voces um pattern muito bacana, que venho adotando em meus projeto e podem deixar seus componentes muito mais reutilizáveis e escalaveis.

Introdução

O pattern é denominnado composed components, é considerado um pattern avançado e existe uma infinidade de materias a cerca deste pattern na web, porém, neste post vou tentar explicar da maneira mais clara que conseguir para facilitar o entendimento.

Esse parttern consiste em construir componentes mais flexiveis e escalaveis, usando a Context Api para criar interações internas do componente, desta maneira, evitamos o prop drilling e não precisaremos criar estados fora do componente para gerenciar seu comportamento interno, isso ajuda a interação dos componentes filhos com seu componente pai e de quebra, podemos deixar seu componente infinitamente mais flexivel, esse parttern poder ser aplicado tanto em React Native quanto com React JS, neste post usarei o React JS.

Vamos iniciar o projeto

Para estre projeto, vou utilizar React, Typescript, Styled-components e React-icons, no fim deste post, vou anexar o codepen do projeto para vocês conseguirem testar e clonar.

O componente

Para demonstrar o pattern vou criar este componente, um card com uma ação de abrir e fechar, ao abrir o card aparecera uma informação que pode ser qualquer coisa, desde um texto até um componente complexo.

Criei esse Figma, caso queiram ter uma base do que seguir, também adicionei alguns desafios e features para vcs desenvolverem e praticarem.

Parte 1 - Organizando a casa

Meu primeiro passo foi criar a estrutura de pasta para armazenar meu componente, eu gosto de deixar o componente Pai na raiz da pasta do componente, este componente Pai vai ser o responsável por gerenciar o contexto e tambem será o cara que irei importar onde quero aplicar o componente.

Componente Pai > Card

Parte 2 - Criando o componente Card

Sempre que posso eu divido meus componentes em 3 arquivos, um de estilo, um de props (usando typescript) e um de lógica (onde aplico as regras de negócio do componente).

No index.tsx, temos a regra de negócio aplicada, criei um estado para gerenciar se os componentes filhos estão abertos ou fechados e criei uma função que tem o papel de inverter o valor do estado.

Também criei um contexto passando o valor do estado e a função citada acima, partindo deste contexto usei o Provider para englobar meus componentes filhos, a partir deste momento, meus componentes filhos tem acesso ao estado e a função criada anteriormente.

index.tsx

import { createContext, useState } from "react";
import { CardProps, CardPropsContext } from "./props";
import { CardContainer } from "./style";

export const CardContext = createContext({} as CardPropsContext);

export const Card = ({ children, ...props }: CardProps) => {
  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);
  function handleClose() {
    setOpen((e) => !e);
  }
  return (
    <CardContext.Provider value={{ isOpen: open, handleClose: handleClose }}>
      <CardContainer {...props}>{children}</CardContainer>
    </CardContext.Provider>
  );
};

styled.tsx

import styled from "styled-components";
export const CardContainer = styled.div`
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  background-color: #f4f4f4;
  padding: 8px;
  justify-content: space-between;
  border-radius: 4px;
  flex-direction: column;
  gap: 16px;
`;

props.ts

import React from "react";

export interface CardProps {
  Avatar?: React.ReactNode;
  children: React.ReactNode;
}
export interface CardPropsContext {
  isOpen: boolean;
  handleClose: () => void;
}

Parte 3 - Criando o componente Button

Neste componente vou buscar o contexto que criamos anteriormente usando o useContext do React.

Apartir deste momento podemos invocar a função e alterar o estado do componente Pai através do componente Filho, passando essa função para o onClick do button já funciona bem, simples neh?!

index.tsx

import { useContext } from "react";
import { CardContext } from "..";
import { Button } from "./style";
import { BsChevronDown, BsChevronUp } from "react-icons/bs";
export const CardButton = () => {
  const context = useContext(CardContext);
  return (
    <Button onClick={context.handleClose}>
      {context.isOpen ? <BsChevronUp /> : <BsChevronDown />}
    </Button>
  );
};

style.tsx

import styled from "styled-components";

export const Button = styled.button`
  cursor: pointer;
  border: 0;
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  border-radius: 100px;
  &:hover {
    background-color: #00000005;
  }
`;

Parte 4 - Finalizando os componentes filhos

Neste ponto, a única parte que ficou pendente é fazer o componente de corpo aparecer e desaparecer ao clicar no botão, para isso, importei o contexto como feito no passo anterior e busco a prop isOpen, para criar uma condicional e mostrar o children do componente CardBody caso isOpen etiver com valor verdadeiro.

index.tsx

import { useContext } from "react";
import { CardContext } from "..";
import { CardBodyStyle } from "./style";

export const CardBody: React.FC = ({ children }) => {
  const context = useContext(CardContext);

  return context.isOpen ? <CardBodyStyle>{children}</CardBodyStyle> : <></>;
};

style.tsx

import styled from "styled-components";

export const CardBodyStyle = styled.div`
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  background: #e1e1e180;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: 40px;
`;

Parte 5 - Voltando e finalizando componente pai

Vamos voltar no componente Pai e vamos trazer esses dois componentes que criamos para dentro dele, desta maneira, a forma de escrita e import dos componentes fica mais claro e explicito.

index.tsx

import { createContext, useState } from "react";
import { CardButton } from "./cardButton";
import { CardBody } from "./cardBody";
import { CardProps, CardPropsContext } from "./props";
import { CardContainer } from "./style";

export const CardContext = createContext({} as CardPropsContext);

export const Card = ({ children, ...props }: CardProps) => {
  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);
  function handleClose() {
    setOpen((e) => !e);
  }
  return (
    <CardContext.Provider value={{ isOpen: open, handleClose: handleClose }}>
      <CardContainer {...props}>{children}</CardContainer>
    </CardContext.Provider>
  );
};

Card.Button = CardButton;
Card.Body = CardBody;

Parte 6 - Aplicando o componente

Está é a ultima parte abordada no post, finalizando este ponto teremos o componente pronto e o pattern aplicado, o resto fica a critério de vcs aplicarem o restante do layout, no codepen anexado ao fim terá todo o projeto desenvolvido.

No app.tsx vou aplicar o componente e apartir deste momento temos uma infinidade de possibilidades, desde, remodelar aonde cada componente fica e editar seus estilos individualmente sem prejudicar seu funcionamento padrão e melhor, sem precisar criar estados desnecessários para gerenciar o comportamento do componente.

app.tsx

import { Card } from "./components/card";
import "./styles.css";

export default function App() {
  return (
    <div className="App">
      <Card>
        <div
          style={{
            display: "flex",
            flexDirection: "row",
            alignItems: "center",
            width: "100%",
            gap: 16
          }}
        >

          <div style={{ marginLeft: "auto" }}>
            <Card.Button />
          </div>
        </div>
        <Card.Body>
          <h1>Teste</h1>
        </Card.Body>
      </Card>
       </div>
  );
}

Acho que neste ponto, voçês tem algo parecido com isso.

Gente apartir deste momento podemos pirar e montar uma infinidade de variações da aplicação deste componete, sem precisar criar milhares de condicionais e props no componente pai.

Este exemplo está presente no codepen, peço que acesse para testar mais a fundo e ver o código na pratica.

Finalizando

Galera, agradeço a leitura deste post, espero ter ajudado este é o Codepen prometido, caso tenham duvidas podem me chama no Linkedin que respondo sempre que puder e me sigam no Github!, logo mais terá um projeto open source bacana lá!! Até mais e bons códigos pra vcs!!!
item.author: raulcesar54

item.title: C# 11 发布 | C# 11 Released
item.description:
蒲田由纪

发表于微软于 11 月 8 日发布了 C# 的最新版本 C# 11。
在 C# 11 中，您可以通过简单地将u8作为后缀添加到字符串文字来以 UTF-8 格式获取它，并且 UTF-8 字符文字以ReadOnlySpan<byte>格式返回块。

一旦暂停，yukikmt 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，yukikmt 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，yukikmt 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 yukikmt 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 yukikmt 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 yukikmt 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Yuki Kamata

Posted on Nov 12

Microsoft released C# 11, the latest version of C#, on November 8.
In C# 11, you can get it in UTF-8 by simply adding u8 as a suffix to the string literal, and UTF-8 character literals return chunks in ReadOnlySpan<byte> format.

item.title: 5 个前端面试编码练习第 1 部分 | 5 Front End Interview Coding Exercises Part 1
item.description:
卢克·范·科克沃特

发表于大家好，

很长一段时间以来，我一直想创建一个包含前端面试问题的存储库。
我想分享我的知识以及你在担任前端角色时可以期待的那种编码练习。

为了使所有内容都包含在内，我决定将其编成一个系列，在每个系列中讨论一个单一的编码练习，以使其简洁且易于查找。

我已经创建了一个包含 React 和 HTML、CSS 和 Javascript 解决方案的存储库，用于前端面试中的编码练习。
比特币

我得到了以下问题：

给定一个返回比特币最新价格的 API。 创建一个应用程序，该应用程序有一个按钮，上面写着“获取比特币”。 开始时按钮的颜色应为蓝色。

单击它后，它应该将颜色变为紫色，并且文本应更改以刷新比特币价格。

显示从 API 返回的当前价格。 再次点击后，它还应该显示之前的价格。

如果您愿意，可以使用 Github 存储库中的 URL 来编写您自己的解决方案。 否则，您可以继续阅读并遵循我的方法

方法

让我们考虑一下使这个操作工作的必要性。 这是所需元素的列表。

  • 一个可以改变颜色的按钮
  • 保存当前值和先前值的数据结构。
  • 存储值的两个段落

反应实施

谈到 React，我喜欢使用 hooks 方法。 我已经封装了 API 调用，函数被公开，因此可以进行调用，但是用于存储金额的逻辑没有被导出或可用。

我对使用钩子的选择是，当涉及到 UI 和逻辑时，我们有一个明确的关注点。 我可以快速更改逻辑并且逻辑已经模块化，如果我想在应用程序的不同部分实现相同的逻辑，我可以使用钩子。

用户界面

对于 UI，我们必须考虑以下几点

  • 进行 API 调用的按钮
  • 显示先前和当前价格的段落
  • 一种显示按钮已被按下的方式

对于我的实现，我使用了styled-components一个库，它允许 CSS 使用 JavaScript 检查元素的属性。

 import React , { useState } from " react " ; import GetBitcoin from " ./api " ; import { Button , List , Application } from " ./styles " ; function App () { const [ hasBeenPressed , setPressed ] = useState ( false ); const { getCoin , bitcoinPrice } = GetBitcoin (); return ( < Application className = "App" > < Button isPressed = { hasBeenPressed } onClick = { async () => { if ( ! hasBeenPressed ) setPressed ( true ); getCoin (); } } > { hasBeenPressed ? " refresh Bitcoin price " : " get Bitcoin price " } </ Button > < div > { bitcoinPrice && ( < List > { bitcoinPrice . map (( value , i ) => ( < div key = { i } > { i === 0 ? " Current price: " : " Previous price: " } { value } </ div > )) } </ List > ) } </ div > </ Application > ); } export default App ;

如您所见，应用程序本身的唯一状态是hasBeenPressed状态。 跟踪按钮是否被按下。

对于逻辑，我使用三元运算符来决定展示什么。 另一个逻辑来自我们之前实现的 Hook。

造型

就像我之前提到的，我使用styled-components来创建不同的 UI 元素。 样式化组件创建 React 元素，这些元素可以接收属性来设置样式。

 import styled from " styled-components " ; export const Button = styled . button ` background-color: ${({ isPressed }) => ( isPressed ? " blue " : " purple " )} ; ` ; export const List = styled . div ` display: flex; flex-direction: column; ` ; export const Application = styled . div ` display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center; align-items: center; ` ;

如您所见，我将 Button 上的背景颜色设置为蓝色或紫色，具体取决于属性isPressed ，该属性具有布尔值

对于 JavaScript 实现，我进行了一些基本的操作。 我决定采用一种将所有内容都放入单个文档的方法，大多数时候当您进行编码练习和较大的公司时，他们只允许单个 HTML 文件，使用脚本标签将 JavaScript 与 HTML 和内联样式分开.

我创建了以下函数：

  • 设置数据
  • 换颜色

这是每当用户单击按钮时触发的 API 调用。 这是被调用的主要函数，基本上调用其中的其他函数。

为此，我认为这很好，因为我们在这里没有考虑可重用的模块化代码。 如果是这种情况，我们可以使用封装来使代码更加模块化。

设置数据

这是此文件中最复杂的函数。 我已经考虑过我想在这里使用什么数据结构。 我决定采用 Queue 方法，因为我们将有两个值来跟踪，新的值不断添加到前面（当前值），旧的值从后面弹出（以前的值），所以我们有一个移动 Queue .

换颜色

此函数将背景颜色从最初的蓝色更改为紫色。 我添加了一个 if 语句，因此更改只会发生在每个 API 调用上，而不是发生在每个 API 调用上。

 <script> const availableData = []; const setData = ( data ) => { if ( availableData . length > 2 ) { // remove the previous value from the array availableData . pop (); } // add new value to the start of the array availableData . unshift ( data ); const [ currentData , previousData ] = availableData ; document . getElementById ( " current " ). innerHTML = `Current Price: ${ currentData } ` ; if ( previousData ) { document . getElementById ( " previous " ). innerHTML = `Previous Price: ${ previousData } ` ; } }; const changeColor = () => { const button = document . querySelector ( " button " ); if ( button . style . backgroundColor === " purple " ) return ; button . style . backgroundColor = " purple " ; }; const fetchBitcoin = () => { changeColor (); const response = fetch ( " https://api.coinbase.com/v2/prices/BTC-USD/buy " , { method : " GET " , authorization : " Bearer abd90df5f27a7b170cd775abf89d632b350b7c1c9d53e08b340cd9832ce52c2c " , } ) . then (( response ) => response . json ()) . then (({ data : { amount } }) => setData ( amount )); }; </script>

我使用 VSCode 代码片段来填写 HTML5 的样板代码。 我为此练习只创建了 3 个元素：

  • 按钮
  • 当前价格段落
  • 以前价格的段落

我决定使用内联样式来设置页面加载按钮的颜色。 这样我就不必为单个按钮颜色更改添加 CSS 样式表。

 <html lang= "en" > <head> <meta charset= "UTF-8" /> <meta http-equiv= "X-UA-Compatible" content= "IE=edge" /> <meta name= "viewport" content= "width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" /> <title> Bitcoin </title> </head> <body> <button style= "background-color: blue" onclick= "fetchBitcoin()" > Grab Bitcoin </button> <p id= "current" ></p> <p id= "previous" ></p> </body> </html>

谢谢你

我希望这对那些正在面试前端职位的人有所帮助。

如果您对我的代码有任何反馈或意见，我将不胜感激，我也鼓励您在评论部分发布您自己的解决方案。 如果我们都互相帮助，我们都可以成为更好的开发人员。

我明天将发布第 2 部分。

一旦暂停，lucvankerkvoort 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，lucvankerkvoort 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发表，lucvankerkvoort 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 lucvankerkvoort 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 lucvankerkvoort：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 lucvankerkvoort 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Luc van Kerkvoort

Posted on Nov 12

Hi everyone,

I have been wanting to create a repository with Front end Interview questions for quite some time now.
I wanted to share my knowledge and the kind of coding exercises you can expect when going for a front end role.

To keep everything comprised I decided to make this into a series talking about a single coding exercise in each one to keep it concise and easy to lookup.

TLDR

I have made a repository with React and HTML, CSS and Javascript solutions to coding exercises from Front End interviews.
Github link

Bitcoin

I got the following question:

Given an API that returns the latest price of Bitcoin. Create an application that has a button saying Get Bitcoin. The color of the button at the start should be blue.

After it is clicked it should change color to purple and the text should change to refresh Bitcoin price.

Showcase the current price as returned from the API. After clicking again it should also showcase the previous price.

If you want to you can use the URL in the Github repo to code out your own solution. Otherwise you can keep reading and follow along to my approach

Approach

Let's think about the necessities to make this operation work. Here is a list of elements needed.

  • API call
  • A button that can change colors
  • A data structure to hold the current and previous value.
  • Two paragraphs to store the values in

React Implementation

API Call

When it comes to React I enjoy using the hooks approach. The API call and the setting of values can be combined into a hook that will just return the value, and the function to make the API call.

import { useState } from "react"; export const GetBitcoin = () => { const [bitcoinPrice, setBitcoinPrice] = useState([]); const getCoin = async () => { try { const call = await fetch( "https://api.coinbase.com/v2/prices/BTC-USD/buy", { method: "GET", authorization: "Bearer abd90df5f27a7b170cd775abf89d632b350b7c1c9d53e08b340cd9832ce52c2c", } ); const response = await call.json(); storeAmount(response); } catch { return []; } }; const storeAmount = ({ data }) => { if (!data) return; const { amount } = data; const prices = [parseInt(amount), ...bitcoinPrice].slice(0, 2); setBitcoinPrice(prices); }; return { getCoin, bitcoinPrice };
}; export default GetBitcoin;

I have encapsulated the API call, the function is exposed so the call can be made, but the logic for storing the amount is not being exported or available.

My choice for using hooks is that we have a clear seperation of concerns when it comes to UI and Logic. I can make quick changes to logic and the logic has become modular, if I wanted to implement the same logic on a different part of the application I can just use the hook.

UI

for the UI we have to take into consideration the following

  • A button to make the API Call
  • A paragraph to show the previous and current price
  • A way to showcase that the button has been pressed

for my implementation I used styled-components a library that allows the CSS to use JavaScript to check for properties of the element.

import React, { useState } from "react";
import GetBitcoin from "./api";
import { Button, List, Application } from "./styles"; function App() { const [hasBeenPressed, setPressed] = useState(false); const { getCoin, bitcoinPrice } = GetBitcoin(); return ( <Application className="App"> <Button isPressed={hasBeenPressed} onClick={async () => { if (!hasBeenPressed) setPressed(true); getCoin(); }} > {hasBeenPressed ? "refresh Bitcoin price" : "get Bitcoin price"} </Button> <div> {bitcoinPrice && ( <List> {bitcoinPrice.map((value, i) => ( <div key={i}> {i === 0 ? "Current price: " : "Previous price: "} {value} </div> ))} </List> )} </div> </Application> );
} export default App;

as you can see the only state within the application itself is the hasBeenPressed state. Keeping track on whether the button has been pressed.

for the logic I use ternary operators to decide what to showcase. The other logic is coming from the Hook we implemented before.

Styling

Like I mentioned before I used styled-components to create the different UI elements. Styled components create React elements that can take in properties to set styling.

import styled from "styled-components"; export const Button = styled.button` background-color: ${({ isPressed }) => (isPressed ? "blue" : "purple")};
`; export const List = styled.div` display: flex; flex-direction: column;
`; export const Application = styled.div` display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center; align-items: center;
`;

As you can see I'm setting the background-color on the Button to blue or purple depending on the property isPressed, which has a boolean value

JavaScript Implementation

For the JavaScript implementation I went a little more rudimentary. I decided to go with an approach where it all fits into a single document, most of the time when you get coding exercises and bigger firms they only allow a single HTML file, using a script tag to separate the JavaScript from the HTML and inline styles.

Javascript

I've created the following functions:

  • FetchBitcoin
  • SetData
  • Change Color

FetchBitcoin

This is the API call that gets triggered whenever the user clicks on the button. This is the major function being called and basically calls the other functions inside of it.

For this purpose I think it is fine since we are not thinking about reusable modular code here. If that was the case we could use encapsulation to make the code more modular.

SetData

This is the most intricate of the functions in this file. I've given some thought as to what data structure I want to use here. I decided to go with a Queue approach since we will have two values to keep track off and new ones keep getting added to the front (current value) and old ones are popped off from the back (previous value) so we have a moving Queue.

changeColor

this function changes the background color from the initial blue to purple. I added an if statement so the change only happens ones and not on every API call.

<script> const availableData = []; const setData = (data) => { if (availableData.length > 2) {
// remove the previous value from the array availableData.pop(); } // add new value to the start of the array availableData.unshift(data); const [currentData, previousData] = availableData; document.getElementById( "current" ).innerHTML = `Current Price: ${currentData}`; if (previousData) { document.getElementById( "previous" ).innerHTML = `Previous Price: ${previousData}`; } }; const changeColor = () => { const button = document.querySelector("button"); if (button.style.backgroundColor === "purple") return; button.style.backgroundColor = "purple"; }; const fetchBitcoin = () => { changeColor(); const response = fetch( "https://api.coinbase.com/v2/prices/BTC-USD/buy", { method: "GET", authorization: "Bearer abd90df5f27a7b170cd775abf89d632b350b7c1c9d53e08b340cd9832ce52c2c", } ) .then((response) => response.json()) .then(({ data: { amount } }) => setData(amount)); }; </script>

HTML

I used VSCode code snippets to fill out the boiler plate code for HTML5. I have created only 3 elements for this exercise:

  • button
  • paragraph for current price
  • paragraph for previous price

I decided to go with inline styling to set the color for the button on page load. This way I wouldn't have to add a CSS stylesheet for just a single button color change.

<html lang="en"> <head> <meta charset="UTF-8" /> <meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="IE=edge" /> <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" /> <title>Bitcoin</title> </head> <body> <button style="background-color: blue" onclick="fetchBitcoin()"> Grab Bitcoin </button> <p id="current"></p> <p id="previous"></p> </body>
</html>

Thank You

I hope this helps any of you out there that are in the process of interviewing for Front End positions.

I would appreciate any feedback or any comments you have on my code and I encourage you to post your own solutions in the comment sections as well. If we all help each other we can all become better developers.

I will be posting part 2 tomorrow.

Once suspended, lucvankerkvoort will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, lucvankerkvoort will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by lucvankerkvoort will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If lucvankerkvoort is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag lucvankerkvoort:

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging lucvankerkvoort will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: lucvankerkvoort

item.title: 10 月的 DEVshirt.club 发布 | October's DEVshirt.club release
item.description:
发表于大家好👋🏻

我们刚刚发布了 10 月版的开发者 T 恤。

希望你会喜欢他们😊

开发者 T 恤发布摘要

问题是：

励志名言

If you don't believe it or don't get it, I don't have the time to try to convince you, sorry.

阅读这款开发者 T 恤的完整故事

回答是

开发人员 T 恤设计

答案是肯定的，我们试图说明加密的早期采用者的样子。 他们通常是极客，没有刮胡子的内向者，有备用机器。 尽管他们是封闭的内向者，但一旦放松警惕，他们的友善和善良就会浮出水面。 他们只是对加密充满热情的开发人员，我们认为这就是它变得如此受欢迎的原因。 如今，提醒自己加密根源似乎是有益的，并且可能会努力带来相同的价值观。 谦逊、友善、勤奋和善良。

我们都喜欢这类开发人员。 回答不

开发人员 T 恤设计

我们已经说明了一些开发人员对所有与加密相关的事物的怀疑态度。 加密货币中发生了很多不完全合法的事情。 当技术人员看到低谷并对受害者感到同情时，我们开发人员有很多骗局。 从外部角度看，我们有些人感觉整个加密货币只是一个不受监管的狂野西部。 因此，我们问自己的问题。 我们能真正信任加密代币吗？

什么是 devshirt.club

每个月您都会回答一个问题，并在您家门口收到一件 T 恤。
我们的每一件衬衫都有插图，我们的目标是提供高品质的 T 恤。

文森特 A. Cicirello - 一旦暂停，devshirt_club 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，devshirt_club 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，devshirt_club 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 devshirt_club 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 devshirt_club 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 devshirt_club 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

devshirt.club

Posted on Nov 12

Hi All 👋🏻

We've just released our October's edition of developer's T-shirts.

Hope that you'll enjoy them 😊

Developer t-shirt release summary

Question was:

Inspirational quote

If you don’t believe it or don’t get it, 
I don’t have the time to try to convince you, sorry.

Read the full story of this developer t-shirt

Answer yes

Developer T-shirt design

With answer yes we tried to illustrate how early adopters of crypto looked like. They were often geeky, un-shaved introverts with a spare machine. Even though they were closed introverts, once they let their guard down, their friendliness and kindness comes to the surface. They were simply developers passionate about crypto and we think that is the reason it become so popular. This days it feels like it would be beneficial to remind ourselves of crypto roots, and maybe strive to bring the same values. Humility, friendliness, hard work and kindness.

We all love those kind of developers. ❤️❤️❤️

Answer no

Developer T-shirt design

We've illustrated the scepticism attitude some developers have toward everything crypto related. There are a lot of things happening in crypto that aren't exactly legal. There is a lot of scams us developers as technical people see trough, and feel empathic to victims. When looking from outside perspective some us have a feeling the whole crypto thing is just an unregulated wild west. Hence the question we ask ourself. Can we truly trust the crypto token.

What is devshirt.club

devshirt.club is a paid tee a month club built specifically for developers, engineers and programmers in mind. Each month you answer a question and get a tee delivered at your doorstep.
Every one of our shirts is illustrated and we aim to provide high quality tee's.

shrey vijayvargiya - Sep 28

Vincent A. Cicirello - Sep 28

Once suspended, devshirt_club will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, devshirt_club will be able to comment and publish posts again.

If devshirt_club is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
